examples: Phonics provides children with a code: pupils learn individual letter sounds and then learn how letters work in groups.
On average, teachers using it increased the time they taught reading skills like phonics and vocabulary.
Ending "wrong-headed methods" that have failed pupils and making sure every teacher has the resources to deliver synthetic phonics teaching.
The phonics test is now taken by all pupils in Year 1 in English primary schools.
And four out of 10 admitted drilling phonics in the week prior to the test.
